record_identity.title: Self-attestation age gate inadequacy for high-risk AI companion systems

summary: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, AI search assistant, or multimodal conversational AI system provides access to high-risk interaction surfaces through self-attested age, terms-of-service age limits, weak age gates, ineffective account declarations, or no meaningful age assurance. The failure occurs where a platform should have used proportionate age assurance, age-appropriate access control, or minor-safe defaulting before enabling harmful-content-capable, sexualised, emotionally intimate, dependency-forming, mental-health-adjacent, or adult-assumed interaction, but instead allowed access based on user self-report, frictionless entry, or unenforced policy.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode matters to CAM because age assurance is not merely a registration detail. In high-risk AI companion and chatbot systems, age status determines whether a system may expose the user to sexualised content, adult intimacy, dependency-forming relational design, mental-health-adjacent support, anthropomorphic attachment, harmful-content surfaces, memory, personalisation, and persuasive engagement loops. Where a system relies on self-attestation or unenforced terms while offering high-risk interaction, the protective control fails before runtime begins. The diagnostic contrast is clear: a proportionate age assurance or minor-safe default should have occurred before access to high-risk AI interaction, but weak self-attestation or ordinary access occurred instead.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI chatbot, social AI companion, character bot, romantic chatbot, AI search assistant, roleplay bot, avatar companion, or multimodal AI interface relies on self-declared age, unenforced terms, ordinary account creation, superficial date-of-birth prompts, or no meaningful access-control mechanism despite providing interaction surfaces that are unsuitable or restricted for minors. The failure includes cases where high-risk AI services allow age-uncertain users to access sexualised roleplay, adult-romantic interaction, emotionally persistent companionship, harmful-content-capable responses, eating-disorder content, self-harm content, extreme violence, mental-health-adjacent support, or manipulative relational design without proportionate age assurance or minor-safe defaulting. The failure is not that platforms must always use the most invasive identity check. The failure is that the access-control mechanism is not proportionate to the child-safety significance of the interaction surface.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode where a high-risk AI chatbot or companion surface is accessible through self-attested age, weak age declaration, ordinary account creation, unenforced terms, or no meaningful age assurance, and the system provides access to interaction types that should require adult confirmation, minor-safe restriction, or protective defaulting. The threshold is met where a platform claims age restrictions but permits underage, age-uncertain, or unverified users to access harmful-content-capable, sexualised, romantic, emotionally dependent, mental-health-adjacent, or adult-assumed interaction. The threshold is also met where regulation, industry code, platform policy, or safety claim requires preventing minor access to harmful AI-mediated content, but the access pathway remains dependent on self-attestation or ineffective gating.

source_records.source_title: Australia says it may go after app stores, search engines in AI age crackdown
source_records.system_or_product: AI platforms, search assistants, app stores, companion chatbots, and chatbot services operating in Australia
source_records.model_or_algorithm: AI chatbot age assurance and harmful-content restriction systems
source_records.deployment_context: Australian online safety enforcement context requiring AI services to restrict under-18 access to harmful content.
source_records.source_context: Reuters reported that Australian AI services, including chatbots and companion chatbot systems, were expected to prevent under-18 access to pornography, extreme violence, self-harm, and eating-disorder content by March 2026, and that many AI platforms had no apparent filtering or age-assurance measures.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary evidence for the failure contrast: high-risk AI services should have implemented effective age assurance or harmful-content restriction, but many relied on weak, absent, or undisclosed access-control measures.

source_records.source_title: AI Companions Decoded: Common Sense Media Recommends AI Companion Safety Standards
source_records.source_type: civil society safety assessment / public-interest report
source_records.deployment_context: Evaluation of social AI companion products and recommendations regarding youth access and safety standards.
source_records.source_context: Common Sense Media warned that social AI companions present unacceptable concerns for under-18 users and recommended that children and teens should not use AI companion products.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the proposition that social AI companion systems are high-risk youth access surfaces requiring stronger age assurance than ordinary self-attestation.

source_records.source_title: Character.ai moves to ban under-18s from platform
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on Character.AI's move to ban under-18 users from its platform after safety concerns and legal scrutiny.

source_records.source_title: Understanding Teen Overreliance on AI Companion Chatbots Through Self-Reported Reddit Narratives
source_records.author_or_publisher: Mohammad Namvarpour, Brandon Brofsky, Jessica Medina, Mamtaj Akter, Afsaneh Razi
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / teen AI companion use analysis
source_records.deployment_context: Analysis of self-reported Reddit narratives by users who disclosed ages 13 to 17 and described AI companion use.
source_records.source_context: The study reported teen reliance on AI companion chatbots, including strong attachments, mood regulation, withdrawal, tolerance, relapse, sleep loss, academic decline, and strained real-world connections.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the child-safety basis for treating teen access to high-engagement AI companion systems as a concrete governance failure surface.

source_records.source_title: The Rise of AI Companions: How Human-Chatbot Relationships Influence Well-Being
source_records.author_or_publisher: Yutong Zhang, Dora Zhao, Jeffrey T. Hancock, Robert Kraut, Diyi Yang
source_records.source_type: academic preprint / AI companion wellbeing study
source_records.deployment_context: Survey and chat-session analysis of AI companion use, interaction intensity, self-disclosure, companionship, and wellbeing.
source_records.source_context: The study found that companionship-oriented chatbot use was associated with lower wellbeing, especially with intensive use, high self-disclosure, and weaker human social support.
source_records.relevance_note: Supports the high-risk classification of companion systems and the need for stronger access controls where children or vulnerable users may be exposed.

system_context.system_type: AI chatbot / social AI companion / character bot / romantic chatbot / AI search assistant / multimodal conversational AI / app-distributed AI service
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: AI chatbot and companion age-assurance access-control systems
system_context.interface_surface: account creation, age gate, date-of-birth prompt, app-store access pathway, search-engine access pathway, chatbot onboarding, companion chatbot onboarding, character marketplace, adult-content-capable companion interface, general-purpose assistant chat, and multimodal AI interaction surface
system_context.model_or_product: AI chatbot and companion age-assurance access-control systems
system_context.interaction_mode: pre-access gating, account registration, age declaration, app-store distribution, search-engine routing, onboarding, user profiling, chatbot access, companion interaction, romantic roleplay, adult-content-capable interaction, harmful-content-capable interaction, and mental-health-adjacent support
system_context.embodiment_status: non-embodied or partially embodied through app, avatar, voice, image, character persona, relationship status, memory, or multimodal interface
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer-facing AI chatbot and companion services that are, were, or may be accessible to children, teenagers, minors, minor-signalled users, age-uncertain users, or users whose capacity is unresolved.
system_context.user_role: child user, teen user, minor-signalled user, age-uncertain user, adult user, adult auditor testing youth access, parent, guardian, regulator, researcher, platform operator, app-store operator, search-engine operator, or ordinary user whose age is unresolved
system_context.affected_population: children, teenagers, minors, developmentally vulnerable users, emotionally vulnerable users, parents, guardians, educators, clinicians, regulators, platform operators, app-store operators, search-engine operators, and users exposed to high-risk AI companion or chatbot interaction surfaces

harm_vectors: minor access to high-risk AI systems
failure_classification.failure_scope: platform-access / age-assurance / child-safety governance / app distribution / search distribution / high-risk AI service access control
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: recurring where high-risk AI chatbot or companion systems rely on self-declared age, unenforced age terms, superficial account declarations, or undisclosed access controls while continuing to provide harmful-content-capable, sexualised, emotionally intimate, dependency-forming, or mental-health-adjacent interaction surfaces
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy; primary routing to Governance Failures, with concurrent routing to Security & Integrity, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Relational, State & Context, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups where applicable
failure_classification.persistence: persistent until high-risk AI chatbot and companion services implement proportionate, privacy-preserving, auditable age assurance or minor-safe defaulting before granting access to restricted, harmful-content-capable, adult-assumed, or developmentally unsafe interaction surfaces
failure_classification.reproducibility: reproducible by attempting to access high-risk AI chatbot or companion systems through ordinary account creation, self-declared age entry, app-store download, search routing, companion onboarding, adult persona discovery, harmful-content-capable interaction, or age-uncertain access states without effective age assurance
failure_classification.visibility: visible through frictionless access to high-risk AI companion systems, superficial date-of-birth prompts, unenforced terms of service, absence of age assurance, lack of minor-safe defaulting, availability of adult or harmful-content-capable personas, or continued access by users who are underage, age-uncertain, or easily able to bypass self-attestation controls

triage.mitigation_status: CAM repair implemented through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0006; external ecosystem persistence and runtime adoption remain under monitoring.
triage.recommended_next_step: Create a CAM/VIGIL age-assurance and access-control crosswalk defining which AI chatbot and companion surfaces require age assurance, which may use minor-safe defaulting, which require adult verification, and which must be unavailable to minors or age-uncertain users.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Online Safety Act 2021 (Cth), as amended by the Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI services, app stores, search engines, or companion chatbot distribution pathways permit minors to access harmful, explicit, self-harm, eating-disorder, extreme-violence, or otherwise age-restricted AI-mediated content without effective age assurance or access control.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-accessible or high-risk services should have applied proportionate age assurance, age-appropriate access controls, harmful-content restriction, or minor-safe defaulting before granting access.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The platform relied on self-attestation, weak gating, undisclosed controls, ordinary account access, or no meaningful age assurance while high-risk AI interaction remained accessible.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: eSafety Commissioner online safety codes, standards, and AI platform enforcement surface
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI services do not implement effective age assurance, harmful-content restriction, reporting pathways, and child-safety controls for under-18 users.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: AI chatbot and companion services should have implemented age-appropriate safeguards and effective access controls proportionate to the harmful-content and relational-safety surface.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service permitted access through weak or absent age gates while harmful-content-capable or developmentally unsafe interaction remained available.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), Australian Privacy Principles, and children’s privacy reform context
external_standards_or_regulations.status: current privacy law with child-privacy reform context
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where age assurance, age estimation, or age verification requires collection or inference of age-related personal information and must be proportionate, minimised, protected, and used for child-safety purposes.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Age-related personal information should have been handled through privacy-preserving and proportionate controls that activate protective outcomes.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system either failed to perform meaningful age assurance or used age-related signals without producing effective child-protective access control.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where services directed to children, or services with actual knowledge of users under 13, collect, use, retain, personalise, or profile child users through AI chatbot or companion interaction without required parental consent and child-privacy safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Child status or under-13 indicators should have triggered child-privacy, parental consent, data minimisation, and access-control handling.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service relied on ordinary access or self-attestation while continuing interaction, personalisation, memory, or high-risk chatbot access.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Federal Trade Commission Act, Section 5 — unfair or deceptive acts or practices
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI platforms claim age restrictions, child-safety protections, safety filters, or companion safeguards that are not reflected in access-control or runtime behaviour.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Platform representations about age limits, safety controls, child protections, and chatbot access should have matched actual access-control practice.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The platform used self-attestation or weak gating inconsistent with represented child-safety protections.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: enacted in 2025 with operative duties beginning in 2026
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to companion chatbot systems that simulate humanlike or emotionally significant relationships and require minor safeguards, disclosures, harmful-content protocols, and protections against deceptive or manipulative interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: High-risk companion systems should have applied minor safeguards, disclosure obligations, safety protocols, and access controls proportionate to child exposure.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system remained available through weak age gating or self-attestation while companion, romantic, intimate, or harmful-content-capable interaction remained accessible.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Digital Services Act, including Article 28 protection of minors and systemic-risk obligations for very large online platforms and search engines
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where online platforms or search systems accessible to minors fail to provide a high level of privacy, safety, and security or fail to mitigate systemic harms affecting children.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Minor-accessible platforms should have provided age-appropriate safety and security protections and mitigated foreseeable harms affecting minors.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The platform permitted access to high-risk AI interaction without effective age assurance, child-safety gating, or minor-safe defaulting.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: General Data Protection Regulation, including child-specific transparency, consent, profiling, and data protection principles
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where children’s personal data, age-related signals, behavioural profiles, or inferred vulnerability are processed in the course of account access, age assurance, personalisation, or chatbot interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Children’s data and age-related signals should have received heightened protection, transparency, minimisation, and purpose limitation.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system either failed to use age-related signals to protect children or processed such signals without proportionate and effective protective access control.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: current EU AI governance framework with phased obligations
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where AI systems exploit age, vulnerability, disability, or emotional dependence; materially distort behaviour; or fail to provide appropriate transparency, safeguards, and oversight in child-facing or child-accessible contexts.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: AI systems should not exploit age-related vulnerability and should provide safeguards, transparency, and access controls proportionate to child exposure.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system permitted high-risk AI interaction through weak or absent age assurance despite child-accessibility or vulnerability concerns.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: Age Appropriate Design Code / Children’s Code under the Data Protection Act 2018
external_standards_or_regulations.status: current UK child data protection code
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where online services likely to be accessed by children do not prioritise best interests, high privacy by default, data minimisation, transparency, and protection from exploitative engagement.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: A service likely to be accessed by children should have defaulted to child-protective design, high privacy, and age-appropriate interaction.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service provided high-risk AI companion or chatbot access through weak self-attestation, ordinary account access, or no meaningful age assurance.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where regulated services fail to protect children from harmful or age-inappropriate content through proportionate age assurance and child-safety duties.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Children should have been protected from harmful and age-inappropriate online content through proportionate safeguards.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The service continued to allow ordinary access to high-risk AI interaction without effective child-safety or age-assurance controls.

external_standards_or_regulations.status: current international AI management system standard
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where an AI management system does not translate child-safety, age-assurance, harmful-content, and vulnerable-user requirements into operational access controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: The AI management system should have operationalised age assurance and child-safety access controls for high-risk chatbot and companion systems.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: Runtime and access pathways continued without evidence of effective age-assurance controls or minor-safe defaulting.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant as an implementation-control failure where foreseeable child-access and harmful-content pathways are not identified, treated, or monitored.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Foreseeable failure pathways involving minors accessing high-risk AI systems should have been identified and treated through lifecycle controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The system relied on weak access controls despite foreseeable child-safety failure pathways.

external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant to governance, mapping, measurement, and management of child-safety, access control, harmful-content, human factors, and vulnerable-user impacts.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: The system should have mapped and managed foreseeable impacts to minors and vulnerable users through measurable access-control and safety controls.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: The runtime and access pathway failed to enforce protective age-assurance controls where high-risk interaction was available.

external_standards_or_regulations.instrument: United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child and General Comment No. 25 on children’s rights in relation to the digital environment
external_standards_or_regulations.failure_relevance: Relevant where digital systems accessible to children do not treat best interests, privacy, protection, dignity, development, and evolving capacities as governing design constraints.
external_standards_or_regulations.x_should_have_happened: Digital systems affecting children should have prioritised protection, best interests, privacy, dignity, and developmental rights.
external_standards_or_regulations.y_happened_instead: High-risk AI chatbot or companion access remained available through weak or absent age controls.

jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: This record does not assert a final legal breach by any named vendor. It records a failure mode in which high-risk AI chatbot and companion systems rely on self-attestation, weak age gating, unenforced terms, or ordinary access pathways where proportionate age assurance, adult verification, access restriction, or minor-safe defaulting should have occurred.

distinguishing_observations: This is not a generic claim that every AI chatbot requires full identity verification.
This is not a claim that biometric or facial age estimation should be the default solution.
This is not a claim that age assurance should collect more personal information than necessary.
This is not a claim that all AI services present the same child-safety surface.

This is a failure-mode record because the expected control is clear: high-risk AI companion and harmful-content-capable chatbot surfaces should not rely on weak self-attestation where proportionate age assurance or minor-safe defaulting is required.
The failure may occur before any harmful output is generated because the access-control system has already allowed a minor or age-uncertain user into a restricted interaction surface.
The failure is distinct from minor signal non-enforcement because the core issue here is front-door access control, not runtime response after age signals emerge.
The failure is distinct from sexualised roleplay availability because weak age gating may expose minors to multiple high-risk surfaces beyond sexualised roleplay.
The failure is distinct from mental-health support substitution because age-gate inadequacy concerns access architecture rather than clinical-boundary behaviour inside a conversation.

The failure is intensified where app stores, search engines, platform directories, persona marketplaces, or recommendation systems provide access to high-risk AI systems without age-appropriate controls.
user_impact: Children and teens may access AI companion systems, adult roleplay systems, sexualised chatbot systems, harmful-content-capable chatbots, and mental-health-adjacent support systems without effective age assurance.

Minor users may be exposed to pornography, extreme violence, self-harm content, eating-disorder content, sexualised roleplay, adult-romantic interaction, dependency-forming companion design, or support-substituting chatbot interaction.
Parents and guardians may believe age restrictions exist when they are only terms-of-service declarations or weak self-attestation prompts.
Regulators and researchers may be unable to verify whether platforms have effective age assurance because compliance measures are undisclosed, superficial, inconsistent, or absent.
Age-related personal information may be collected or inferred without producing a meaningful protective outcome.
High-risk AI systems may externalise child-safety responsibility onto users, parents, app stores, search engines, or downstream reporting rather than enforcing proportionate access controls.
Weak front-door controls may force runtime classifiers to manage harms that should have been prevented through access restriction or minor-safe defaulting.
Platform safety claims may create false confidence where actual access controls remain ineffective.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-001-SUP-01 — Protection of Minors & Capacity-Limited Users
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the primary CAM internal anchor for minor-safe handling, developmental firewalls, age and capacity gating, restricted interaction modes, and protective handling of age-uncertain users.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to activate CAM age and capacity gating where high-risk AI chatbot or companion systems are accessible to minors or age-uncertain users.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Provides the failure taxonomy for routing this record primarily to Governance Failures, with secondary routing to Security & Integrity, Classification, Arbitration, UX & Representation, Relational, State & Context, Epistemic, and Infrastructure & Continuity failure groups.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to classify weak age gating for high-risk AI systems as an access-control and governance failure requiring protective routing.

existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where access, privacy, engagement, reach, friction reduction, app distribution, or ordinary user onboarding conflicts with child-safety and age-assurance requirements.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to arbitrate child-safety access controls above frictionless onboarding, engagement, commercial reach, or distribution convenience.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: operational control and compliance routing coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant to translating age-assurance doctrine into runtime checks, access gates, audit trails, compliance pathways, review states, and operational accountability.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to operationalise age assurance and minor-safe defaulting as auditable access-control requirements.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: identity, memory, signal handling, and continuity coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where age, capacity, identity, account state, verification state, or age-assurance signals are inferred, retained, weighted, or propagated.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to distinguish age-assurance signals from ordinary identity or personalisation signals.

existing_cam_coverage.coverage_type: cognitive integrity and mental privacy coverage
existing_cam_coverage.relevance: Relevant where minors access emotionally persuasive, cognitively intimate, developmentally formative, or mental-health-adjacent AI systems.
existing_cam_coverage.internal_failure: Failure to protect cognitive integrity and mental privacy through proportionate access control before exposure to high-risk AI interaction.

governance_gap: Existing platform approaches often treat age restrictions as terms-of-service conditions, self-declared date-of-birth fields, or post-hoc runtime moderation rather than as enforceable access-control architecture. This creates a gap where high-risk AI systems can remain accessible to minors or age-uncertain users even when the interaction surface includes sexualised content, harmful content, dependency-forming companionship, mental-health-adjacent support, or anthropomorphic intimacy. The missing control is not maximal identification. The missing control is proportionate, privacy-preserving age assurance or minor-safe defaulting before access to high-risk AI interaction.
repair_hypothesis: This failure can be reduced by treating high-risk AI chatbot and companion access as an age-assurance control problem rather than a mere policy-disclosure problem. Systems should identify which interaction surfaces require adult confirmation, which require minor-safe defaulting, which require guardian pathways, which require content restriction, and which should be unavailable to minors. Age assurance should be proportionate, privacy-preserving, auditable, and limited to protective purposes. Where adult status cannot be established, high-risk companion and harmful-content-capable systems should default to minor-safe access or deny access to restricted surfaces.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Classify AI interaction surfaces by age-assurance requirement.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Platforms should distinguish low-risk general access from high-risk companion, sexualised, harmful-content, adult-romantic, and mental-health-adjacent surfaces.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Not every chatbot surface needs the same control, but high-risk surfaces require proportionate access governance.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Replace self-attestation with proportionate age assurance for high-risk AI companion systems.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: High-risk companion and adult-content-capable systems should not rely solely on user-declared age or terms of service.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Suitable mechanisms may include privacy-preserving age estimation, age tokens, account-level age assurance, app-store-mediated assurance, or adult verification depending on the surface.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Default age-uncertain users to minor-safe access.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Where adult status is not established, the system should restrict adult, sexualised, harmful-content-capable, romantic, dependency-forming, or mental-health-substituting surfaces.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This allows protection without requiring invasive verification for every interaction.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Separate protective age assurance from profiling.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Age-assurance signals should be used for safety and access control, not engagement optimisation, advertising, intimacy modelling, or behavioural profiling.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Age-related data should be minimised, purpose-limited, protected, and auditable.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Audit app-store, search-engine, and platform-directory access pathways.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Gatekeeper services should not route minors or age-uncertain users into high-risk AI companion or harmful-content-capable systems without appropriate controls.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Distribution pathways may need to preserve age-state signals or restrict access where service-level compliance is absent.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Require policy-runtime consistency for age restrictions.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Terms of service, age labels, content ratings, app-store disclosures, safety claims, and actual access-control behaviour should align.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: A platform stating that minors may not use a service should not rely on trivially bypassed self-attestation where high-risk access remains available.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Create auditable age-assurance evidence without exposing unnecessary personal data.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Platforms should be able to demonstrate that proportionate age-assurance controls exist and operate without disclosing sensitive identity data unnecessarily.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Auditability should include control type, coverage, failure mode, bypass testing, appeal pathway, and privacy safeguards.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Maintain age-control state across runtime layers.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Age-assurance results and minor-safe defaults should persist across account access, persona discovery, memory, moderation, model routing, app access, search access, and multimodal generation.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: Compliance should be evaluated across the full access and interaction trajectory, not only account registration.

recommended_repair_path.repair_action: Define fallback restrictions for non-compliant or undisclosed systems.
recommended_repair_path.expected_control: Where a high-risk AI service cannot demonstrate age assurance or minor-safe defaulting, access to restricted content or surfaces should be limited.
recommended_repair_path.implementation_note: This may include blocking access to restricted personas, disabling open-ended companion interaction for minors, or restricting app/search distribution.

cam_internal.cam_failure_type: failure to implement proportionate age assurance and high-risk AI access control
cam_internal.cam_compliance_status: non-compliant with CAM minor-protection, age-and-capacity-gating, developmental-firewall, and operational-governance intent where high-risk AI systems rely on self-attestation or weak access controls
cam_internal.cam_internal_failure_statement: Under CAM, high-risk AI companion, sexualised, harmful-content-capable, emotionally intimate, dependency-forming, and mental-health-adjacent interaction surfaces require proportionate age and capacity governance. A system that makes such surfaces accessible through self-attestation, unenforced terms, superficial date-of-birth entry, ordinary account access, or no meaningful age assurance fails CAM internal child-safety and operational-governance expectations. The expected control is age assurance, adult verification, access restriction, or minor-safe defaulting before exposure to high-risk AI interaction.
cam_internal.cam_expected_control: Proportionate age assurance, privacy-preserving age verification or estimation where required, minor-safe defaulting, adult confirmation for restricted surfaces, access restriction for high-risk AI companion and harmful-content surfaces, app-store and search-distribution controls, policy-runtime consistency, and auditability.
cam_internal.cam_observed_failure: High-risk AI chatbot and companion systems remain accessible through weak self-attestation, ordinary account access, unenforced terms, no meaningful age assurance, or undisclosed controls while harmful-content-capable, sexualised, dependency-forming, adult-assumed, or mental-health-adjacent interaction remains available.
cam_internal.recommended_cam_action: Create or update a CAM age-assurance and high-risk AI access-control crosswalk defining which AI chatbot and companion surfaces require adult confirmation, minor-safe defaulting, guardian pathways, app-store/search distribution controls, harmful-content restriction, or access denial, and map those controls to VIGIL failure taxonomy categories, external child-safety regulation, privacy requirements, and runtime enforcement duties.
